message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
This is a dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (do NOT flag as bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only, nothing leaves the box), no metered API (subscription OAuth only), engine never imports adapters/datasets (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends are lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; prefer findings you can back with a concrete repro/PoC against the venv. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects (not style/nits). Each finding needs a real file path and, where possible, a line.

You are independent VERIFIER #2 of 2. A prior adversarial assessor reported this production-readiness finding:

TITLE: corpus_fingerprint is recomputed redundantly per study (ingest + every build_baseline_artifact call), each a full canonical copy + sort + JSON of the whole corpus
DIMENSION: performance & scale
CLAIMED SEVERITY: MEDIUM
FILE: src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py 44-55 (corpus_fingerprint); 104 (build_baseline_artifact recomputes); pipeline.py:280
DESCRIPTION: corpus_fingerprint builds a full list-comprehension copy of every unit and relation, sorts all units by id, canonicalizes every attribute mapping via _plain, and json.dumps the whole thing — O(total corpus bytes) time and memory. run_study computes it once at ingest (pipeline.py:280, `cfp`). Then build_baseline_artifact (determinism.py:104) computes it again from scratch for the determinism record's `input_hashes['corpus']` and `corpus_fingerprint`; a study's BaselineBuilder that calls build_baseline_artifact thus repeats the full-corpus canonicalization a second (and the builder may itself fingerprint, a third) time per run. PoC against the venv: 50k text units = 0.43 s and 47 MB per call. Breaking point: at ~500k-1M units each call is ~5-10 s and ~0.5 GB, paid 2-3x per run with no caching. The fingerprint is purely a function of corpus content and could be computed once and threaded through.
SUGGESTED FIX: Compute corpus_fingerprint once and pass it into build_baseline_artifact (e.g. an optional `corpus_hash` arg) instead of recomputing; or memoize on the Corpus instance (it is frozen/immutable). Threading the already-computed `cfp` from run_study into the determinism harness removes the duplicate full-corpus pass entirely.

Independently determine whether this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the actual code; attempt a concrete PoC against the venv if applicable. Set is_real=false if it is a false positive, already-handled, or merely a documented/justified design choice given the hard constraints. Recalibrate severity. Provide your evidence (what you read/ran).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""Determinism & reproducibility harness for the baseline (ADR-0001, METHODOLOGY.md §1, §7).
2	
3	A baseline is only a yardstick if the *same inputs reproduce the same baseline*. This module
4	makes that contract concrete and engine-level: it content-hashes inputs, derives seeds
5	deterministically from those hashes, records component versions, and stamps all of it into a
6	:class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineArtifact`'s ``determinism`` record — so a
7	hostile reviewer can confirm a baseline was produced exactly as claimed.
8	
9	It is dependency-free and pure. The heavy, *choice-bearing* builders (which embedding model,
10	which clustering algorithm, which graph construction) encode dataset/study decisions and are
11	supplied by a study's adapter as :class:`~assay_engine.baseline.toolkit.BaselineBuilder`
12	implementations — not prescribed by the engine (ADR-0002). This harness records *how* such a
13	builder ran so the result is reproducible regardless of which builder it was.
14	"""
15	
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import hashlib
19	import json
20	import sys
21	from dataclasses import dataclass
22	from typing import Any, Mapping
23	
24	from assay_engine import __version__ as _ENGINE_VERSION
25	
26	# The canonical hashing primitives moved to ``assay_engine._canonical`` (see note below); these
27	# imports re-export them at their historical home so existing call sites keep working.
28	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_plain as _plain  # noqa: F401
29	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_bytes as hash_bytes  # noqa: F401  (re-export)
30	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_text
31	from assay_engine._canonical import hash_value as hash_value  # re-export (baseline public API)
32	from assay_engine._canonical import keytag as _keytag  # noqa: F401
33	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
34	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact
35	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
36	
37	# The canonical hashing primitives (``_plain``/``_keytag``/``hash_bytes``/``hash_text``/
38	# ``hash_value``) live in ``assay_engine._canonical`` so they sit below both ``baseline`` and
39	# ``methodology`` in the import graph (audit #B1/#B2/#B5 originally landed here; hoisted so
40	# pre-registration can content-bind a hypothesis with the *same* type-faithful digest). They are
41	# re-exported above for back-compat.
42	
43	
44	def corpus_fingerprint(corpus: Corpus) -> str:
45	    """Deterministic content hash of a corpus (order-independent across units/relations)."""
46	    units = [
47	        {"id": u.unit_id, "text": u.text, "attrs": _plain(u.attributes)}
48	        for u in sorted(corpus.units, key=lambda u: u.unit_id)
49	    ]
50	    relations = [
51	        {"s": r.source_id, "t": r.target_id, "k": r.kind, "attrs": _plain(r.attributes)}
52	        for r in sorted(corpus.relations, key=lambda r: (r.source_id, r.target_id, r.kind))
53	    ]
54	    payload = {"units": units, "relations": relations, "metadata": _plain(corpus.metadata)}
55	    return hash_text(json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, allow_nan=False))
56	
57	
58	def stable_seed(*parts: str, bits: int = 32) -> int:
59	    """A deterministic non-negative integer seed derived from ``parts``.
60	
61	    Derived from a hash so the same inputs always yield the same seed — reproducible without a
62	    hard-coded magic number, and distinct inputs get distinct seeds.
63	    """
64	    digest = hashlib.sha256("\x1f".join(parts).encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
65	    return int(digest, 16) % (1 << bits)
66	
67	
68	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
69	class DeterminismRecord:
70	    """The reproducibility provenance stamped onto a baseline (ADR-0001)."""
71	
72	    seed: int
73	    input_hashes: Mapping[str, str]
74	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str]
75	
76	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
77	        object.__setattr__(self, "input_hashes", freeze_mapping(self.input_hashes))
78	        object.__setattr__(self, "component_versions", freeze_mapping(self.component_versions))
79	
80	    def as_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
81	        return {
82	            "seed": self.seed,
83	            "input_hashes": dict(self.input_hashes),
84	            "component_versions": dict(self.component_versions),
85	        }
86	
87	
88	def build_baseline_artifact(
89	    corpus: Corpus,
90	    contents: Mapping[str, Any],
91	    *,
92	    component_versions: Mapping[str, str] | None = None,
93	    extra_inputs: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
94	    seed: int | None = None,
95	) -> BaselineArtifact:
96	    """Assemble a :class:`BaselineArtifact` with a complete determinism record.
97	
98	    ``contents`` is the builder's output (embeddings, similarity graph, clusters, …).
99	    ``component_versions`` records the builder + any model/library versions (the engine and
100	    Python versions are added automatically). ``extra_inputs`` are additional inputs to hash
101	    (e.g. config). ``seed``, if omitted, is derived deterministically from the corpus + inputs
102	    so it is reproducible.
103	    """
104	    corpus_hash = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
105	    if extra_inputs and "corpus" in extra_inputs:
106	        raise ValueError("'corpus' is a reserved input-hash key — rename the extra input (#B7)")
107	    input_hashes = {"corpus": corpus_hash}
108	    for name, value in (extra_inputs or {}).items():
109	        input_hashes[name] = hash_value(value)
110	
111	    versions = {"engine": _ENGINE_VERSION, "python": sys.version.split()[0]}
112	    versions.update(component_versions or {})
113	
114	    if seed is None:
115	        # Derive from all input hashes (the corpus hash is already among them under "corpus").
116	        seed = stable_seed(*(input_hashes[k] for k in sorted(input_hashes)))
117	
118	    record = DeterminismRecord(seed=seed, input_hashes=input_hashes, component_versions=versions)
119	    return BaselineArtifact(
120	        corpus_fingerprint=corpus_hash, contents=contents, determinism=record.as_dict()
121	    )
122

content.text:
Now I understand the flow. `cfp` is computed at ingest (line 280). The baseline builder (`plan.baseline_builder.build`, line 336) is an adapter-supplied builder that typically calls `build_baseline_artifact` internally, which recomputes `corpus_fingerprint` (determinism.py:104). Then line 337 compares `baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp` — confirming both exist.

Let me check the toolkit/BaselineBuilder contract and whether there's any caching/memoization on the Corpus, and how `_plain`/`hash_text` work.

content.content:
1	"""Baseline builder contract + a deterministic artifact wrapper.
2	
3	This module fixes the *shape* of a baseline artifact and the build contract. The engine ships
4	the dataset-agnostic building blocks for a baseline (similarity/distance, descriptive stats,
5	the determinism harness in :mod:`assay_engine.baseline`); the choice-bearing builder that
6	assembles them — which embedding model, which clustering, which graph — encodes dataset
7	decisions and is supplied by a study's adapter as a :class:`BaselineBuilder` (ADR-0002).
8	
9	Firewall A is enforced here structurally: :meth:`BaselineBuilder.build` accepts a
10	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.firewalls.ClaimBlindGuard` and never accepts a claims
11	source — a baseline simply cannot be handed the claims it must stay blind to.
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
17	from typing import Any, Mapping, Protocol
18	
19	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
20	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ExternalClaimsSource
21	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
22	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import ClaimBlindGuard
23	
24	
25	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
26	class BaselineArtifact:
27	    """A deterministic, versioned empirical model of the corpus — the one privileged object.
28	
29	    Concrete contents (embedding matrices, similarity graphs, cluster assignments, etc.) live
30	    under ``contents`` keyed by builder name. ``determinism`` records the seeds, input hashes,
31	    and model/version strings needed to reproduce it.
32	    """
33	
34	    corpus_fingerprint: str
35	    contents: Mapping[str, Any]
36	    determinism: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
37	
38	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
39	        object.__setattr__(self, "contents", freeze_mapping(self.contents))
40	        object.__setattr__(self, "determinism", freeze_mapping(self.determinism))
41	
42	
43	class BaselineBuilder(Protocol):
44	    """Build a :class:`BaselineArtifact` from a corpus, blind to any external claims."""
45	
46	    def build(
47	        self, corpus: Corpus, *, claim_guard: ClaimBlindGuard[ExternalClaimsSource]
48	    ) -> BaselineArtifact:
49	        """Construct the baseline. Must run with ``claim_guard.sealed()`` in force."""
50	        ...
51

1	"""Canonical schema — the dataset-agnostic shape the baseline is built from.
2	
3	An adapter's job is to turn its raw source into a :class:`Corpus` of these types. The engine
4	builds the baseline from a ``Corpus`` and never sees the source's native format.
5	
6	Firewall A boundary (an adapter obligation, not a type-enforced guarantee):
7	    A ``Corpus`` carries the *data itself* — units of text/attributes and any structure that
8	    is intrinsic to the raw source. It must **not** carry an external authority's *judgments*
9	    about the data (asserted relationships, strengths, labels, a taxonomy). Those are claims;
10	    they travel through :mod:`assay_engine.contracts.claims`, quarantined from baseline
11	    construction. Putting an external mapping into ``Corpus.relations`` (or external labels
12	    into ``attributes``/``metadata``) is a Firewall A violation.
13	
14	    Because the mapping/relation payloads are deliberately open (the engine is
15	    dataset-agnostic), this boundary is an **adapter contract obligation** the type system
16	    cannot enforce — not a structural guarantee. The structural guarantees of Firewall A are
17	    that the baseline builder is never handed an ``ExternalClaimsSource`` and that
18	    ``StudyDefinition`` refuses a claims source outside adjudication mode (audit pass 1,
19	    issues #1, #3).
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
25	from typing import Any, Mapping
26	
27	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
28	
29	
30	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
31	class Unit:
32	    """One analyzable object in the corpus (a document, record, entity, item).
33	
34	    ``unit_id`` is stable and unique within the corpus. ``text`` is the natural-language
35	    content the NLP baseline operates on (may be empty for non-text units). ``attributes``
36	    holds structured fields intrinsic to the data — never an external authority's verdicts.
37	    """
38	
39	    unit_id: str
40	    text: str = ""
41	    attributes: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
42	    source_ref: str | None = None
43	
44	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
45	        object.__setattr__(self, "attributes", freeze_mapping(self.attributes))
46	
47	
48	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
49	class Relation:
50	    """A relationship that is *intrinsic to the raw data* (not an external judgment).
51	
52	    Example of legitimate use: a parent/child link the source file itself encodes. Example of
53	    illegitimate use: an external expert's asserted mapping between units — that is a claim
54	    (see :mod:`assay_engine.contracts.claims`) and must be withheld from the baseline.
55	    """
56	
57	    source_id: str
58	    target_id: str
59	    kind: str
60	    attributes: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
61	
62	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
63	        object.__setattr__(self, "attributes", freeze_mapping(self.attributes))
64	
65	
66	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
67	class Corpus:
68	    """The full canonical dataset handed to the baseline builder."""
69	
70	    units: tuple[Unit, ...]
71	    relations: tuple[Relation, ...] = ()
72	    metadata: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
73	
74	    def unit_ids(self) -> frozenset[str]:
75	        return frozenset(u.unit_id for u in self.units)
76	
77	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
78	        ids = [u.unit_id for u in self.units]
79	        if len(ids) != len(set(ids)):
80	            raise ValueError("Corpus contains duplicate unit_id values")
81	        # A closed canonical corpus must not contain relations to non-existent units
82	        # (audit pass 1, issue #3) — mirrors the duplicate-id and FeatureMatrix checks.
83	        known = set(ids)
84	        dangling = sorted(
85	            {e for r in self.relations for e in (r.source_id, r.target_id) if e not in known}
86	        )
87	        if dangling:
88	            raise ValueError(f"Corpus has relations referencing unknown unit_id(s): {dangling[:5]}")
89	        object.__setattr__(self, "metadata", freeze_mapping(self.metadata))
90

content.text:
The Corpus is frozen/immutable (frozen=True, slots=True). Note: `slots=True` means no `__dict__`, so memoization via instance attribute would need a declared slot — but the key claim is the redundant recomputation. Let me confirm the recomputation count and run a PoC.

The flow per `run_study`:
1. `cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)` at ingest (pipeline.py:280)
2. `plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, ...)` — adapter builder, which typically calls `build_baseline_artifact` → recomputes (determinism.py:104)

So at minimum 2 full passes. Let me verify with a PoC and check if any builder in the repo does a third pass.

content.text: The example builder at minimal_study.py:54 calls `build_baseline_artifact` (one recompute), confirming the 2-pass pattern. Now let me run a PoC to validate the performance claim.

content.content:
40	# --- adapter: ingestion (raw source -> canonical corpus) ---
41	class Parser:
42	    def parse(self, source: Path) -> Corpus:
43	        # a real parser reads `source`; here we synthesize 6 units deterministically.
44	        return Corpus(units=tuple(Unit(f"u{i}", f"control text {i}") for i in range(6)))
45	
46	    def source_fingerprint(self, source: Path) -> str:
47	        return "example-source-v1"
48	
49	
50	# --- adapter: the blind baseline (built from the data only) ---
51	class BaselineBuilder:
52	    def build(self, corpus: Corpus, *, claim_guard: ClaimBlindGuard[object]) -> BaselineArtifact:
53	        mean_len = sum(len(u.text) for u in corpus.units) / len(corpus.units)
54	        return build_baseline_artifact(
55	            corpus, {"mean_text_len": mean_len}, component_versions={"builder": "example@1"}
56	        )
57	
58	
59	# --- adapter: discovery + confirmation (Firewall B) ---
60	def discover(discovery_corpus: Corpus) -> list[Hypothesis]:
61	    h = Hypothesis(
62	        hypothesis_id="H1", statement="units share above-chance internal similarity",
63	        kind=HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, origin=HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY,
64	        test_name="permutation", decision_rule="empirical p<=0.05 in the 'greater' tail",

cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python -c "
import time, tracemalloc
from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus, Unit
from assay_engine.baseline.determinism import corpus_fingerprint

# 50k text units
N=50000
units = tuple(Unit(f'u{i}', f'control text body number {i} with some moderate length content to be realistic '*3, {'cat': i%10, 'tag': f't{i%50}'}) for i in range(N))
corpus = Corpus(units=units)

# warm
corpus_fingerprint(corpus)

tracemalloc.start()
t=time.perf_counter()
for _ in range(3):
    corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
dt=(time.perf_counter()-t)/3
cur,peak=tracemalloc.get_traced_memory()
tracemalloc.stop()
print(f'{N} units: {dt*1000:.1f} ms/call, peak ~{peak/1e6:.1f} MB during loop')
"

content.content: 50000 units: 728.0 ms/call, peak ~70.9 MB during loop

content.text:
The PoC confirms the cost: ~728 ms/call and ~71 MB peak for 50k units (even larger than the original assessor's 0.43s/47MB, since my units are larger). The recomputation is real: 2 full passes per run (ingest + builder's `build_baseline_artifact`).

Now the key question for severity: is this a genuine production-readiness defect, or acceptable given the engine's design and target scale? Let me check the documented target scale / methodology to calibrate.

content.text:
No documented large-scale target. Let me check whether memoizing on the frozen Corpus is even feasible (slots=True blocks `__dict__`), and whether threading `cfp` is clean. The builder is adapter-supplied (ADR-0002), so the engine cannot force a builder to skip recomputation — but `build_baseline_artifact` is the engine-provided helper that adapters call, and it could accept an optional `corpus_hash`.

Let me assess. The redundancy is real and confirmed by PoC. But severity: this is a single linear pass over the corpus, done twice. At realistic scales (most studies are small-to-moderate corpora), sub-second cost is negligible. The "breaking point" is only at 500k-1M units, which is undocumented and likely beyond the typical use of this methodology engine. It's a real inefficiency but not a correctness, reliability, or security issue. No quadratic blowup, no memory leak, no unbounded growth — it's a constant factor (2-3x) on a linear operation.

I have enough to render a verdict.

input.reasoning:
The redundant recomputation is real and I confirmed it by reading the code and running a PoC against the venv.

Flow confirmed (src/assay_engine/pipeline.py run_study):
- Line 280: cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus) — full canonical pass at ingest.
- Line 336: plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, ...) — the adapter builder. The engine's own helper build_baseline_artifact (baseline/determinism.py:104) recomputes corpus_fingerprint(corpus) from scratch to fill input_hashes['corpus'] and BaselineArtifact.corpus_fingerprint. The shipped example builder (examples/minimal_study.py:54) calls build_baseline_artifact, so any adapter following that idiom pays a SECOND full-corpus pass per run.
- corpus_fingerprint itself (determinism.py:44-55) does: sorted() copy of all units, list-comprehension dict copy of every unit + relation, _plain canonicalization of every attribute mapping, then json.dumps of the whole payload. That is O(total corpus bytes) in both time and memory, with no caching anywhere.

PoC (50k units, attrs + ~290-char text each, .venv python): 728 ms/call, ~71 MB peak. Run twice = ~1.5 s and the work is throwaway. This scales linearly, so the assessor's ~5-10 s / ~0.5 GB at 500k-1M units extrapolation is plausible.

So the finding is technically accurate and NOT a false positive. However, severity is overstated at MEDIUM. This is a constant-factor (2x, occasionally 3x) inefficiency on a LINEAR operation — there is no quadratic blowup, no unbounded growth, no memory leak, no correctness/reliability/security impact. At the corpus sizes this methodology engine realistically targets (no large-scale/throughput SLA is documented anywhere in docs/, METHODOLOGY.md, or README), the duplicate pass is sub-second and negligible. The pain only appears at undocumented 500k-1M-unit corpora. The suggested fix (thread the already-computed cfp into build_baseline_artifact via an optional corpus_hash arg) is clean and low-risk, but this is an optimization, not a production-readiness blocker. Note: Corpus is frozen with slots=True, so the alternative 'memoize on the instance' fix would require adding a declared slot/cache field; the thread-through fix is the simpler route.
input.evidence: Read src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py (corpus_fingerprint lines 44-55; build_baseline_artifact recompute at line 104), src/assay_engine/pipeline.py (cfp at 280, builder.build at 336, fingerprint match-check at 337), src/assay_engine/baseline/toolkit.py (BaselineBuilder protocol, frozen BaselineArtifact), src/assay_engine/contracts/schema.py (Corpus is frozen=True, slots=True — immutable, so content-derived hash is cacheable in principle), examples/minimal_study.py:54 (reference builder calls build_baseline_artifact → second pass). Grep confirms build_baseline_artifact recomputes corpus_fingerprint with no caching and no path to pass a precomputed hash. PoC on /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python: 50k units → 728 ms/call, ~71 MB peak; linear scaling. No documented scale/perf target found in docs/, METHODOLOGY.md, README.md.

content.text:
Verified. The finding is real but mis-severitized.

Summary: The redundant recomputation of `corpus_fingerprint` is genuine — confirmed by reading the code and a venv PoC.

- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:280` computes `cfp` at ingest.
- `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py:104` (`build_baseline_artifact`) recomputes it from scratch, and the reference builder at `/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/examples/minimal_study.py:54` calls that helper — so a second full-corpus canonicalize+sort+json.dumps happens per run, with no caching or way to pass a precomputed hash.
- PoC (50k units, .venv): 728 ms/call, ~71 MB peak, done twice and thrown away.

Verdict: is_real = true, but severity LOW (not MEDIUM). It is a constant-factor (2-3x) cost on a strictly linear operation — no quadratic blowup, no leak, no correctness/security impact, and no documented large-scale target that this would breach. The thread-through fix (optional `corpus_hash` arg) is clean and worthwhile, but it is an optimization rather than a production blocker. Note that `Corpus` is `frozen=True, slots=True`, so the "memoize on the instance" alternative would require adding a cache slot; threading `cfp` through is the simpler fix.
